finding: Real TestFlight purchase/restore and App Store Connect state remain external launch gates
evidence: Release checklist and iOS release workflow require paywall_testflight_verified before App Store submission
recommended_action: Collect account-owner sandbox/TestFlight purchase and restore proof; confirm metadata, subscriptions, privacy, and public listing state
validation: iOS Release Loop run; TestFlight purchase/restore evidence; App Store Connect proof

finding: Fresh local native proof is blocked until ignored config files are bootstrapped
evidence: Config.xcconfig and Config-Development.xcconfig are ignored and absent before pnpm ios:bootstrap-local-config
recommended_action: Run bootstrap before native proof and keep failures classified as environment until compile begins

finding: Forced Supabase/photo/export URL crash risk has been materially reduced
evidence: SupabaseURLBuilder and focused tests cover malformed URL/config states; targeted rg for URL(string: ...)! has no release-path matches
recommended_action: Keep typed URL builders and malformed-config tests in the release gate

finding: Runtime frame and hitch budgets are not currently proven by reliable trace evidence
evidence: Static audits pass; launch-quality shards now fail empty XCTest bundles; post-merge CI run 28012399897 is the latest landed CI proof; no reliable frame/hitch trace is captured
recommended_action: Capture physical-device or reliable simulator trace before enforcing frame budgets
validation: quality gate and performance-audit workflows with non-empty cases; Instruments or ETTrace artifact

finding: Supabase migration ownership has been consolidated to the root tree but needs a guard to prevent regression
evidence: supabase/migrations is authoritative; apps/web/supabase/migrations is absent on this branch; pnpm check:supabase-migrations reports no unsafe drift
recommended_action: Keep root-only migration creation and drift detection in the release gate

finding: Auth, sync, Core Data, RevenueCat, HealthKit, dashboard, and entry flows are concentrated in large files
evidence: Many Swift files exceed 500 LOC including ExportDataView, AddEntrySheet, MainTabView, RevenueCatManager, RealtimeSyncManager, AuthManager, HealthKitManager, CoreDataManager
recommended_action: Extract policy/data ports and view sections incrementally without changing behavior
validation: Existing unit tests plus focused new tests per extracted seam

finding: Web coverage is still too low for broad product confidence but production-adjacent sync coverage improved
evidence: coverage summary totals: lines 726/6561, statements 755/7014, functions 142/1367, branches 320/4075
recommended_action: Continue adding focused tests for production-adjacent legal, support, account, API, and sync surfaces

finding: No debug/test routes are mounted on this branch, but the middleware blocklist remains a readiness guard against reintroduction
evidence: route-inventory.json has zero debug/test pages or API handlers; middleware-production-gate.test.ts verifies the mounted inventory is empty and known debug/test patterns return 404 before auth
recommended_action: Keep the source-derived route inventory test so new debug/test routes fail loudly before release

finding: Removed synchronous Core Data API runtime traps have been materially reduced
evidence: rg finds no runtime call sites for removed sync APIs; removed-sync bodies no longer call fatalError
recommended_action: Keep removed sync APIs unavailable and rerun call-site scans when Core Data manager changes

finding: OTP/chart/metric force unwrap crash risk has been materially reduced
evidence: Focused scan for last!, weight!, bodyFatPercentage!, ffmi!, and leanMass! is clean in targeted app code
recommended_action: Keep optional metric and OTP edge cases covered as UI surfaces change
validation: Focused UI/unit tests for empty input and missing metrics; force-unwrap scan

finding: Vendor SDK imports are guarded by a static boundary check
evidence: pnpm check:vendor-boundaries scans Swift and TS/JS imports and fails direct vendor SDK imports outside approved service, port, or adapter paths; shared-lib test runs the guard under pnpm test
recommended_action: Keep vendor SDK calls behind internal services, ports, and adapters

finding: GLP-1, DEXA/BodySpec, bulk photo import, AI-adjacent insights, and web DEXA parsing increase surface area beyond Phase 0
evidence: Product roadmap says the launch wedge is paid iOS body-composition HUD with minimal input; web parse-PDF APIs are now Clerk-protected by middleware tests
recommended_action: Keep iOS advanced surfaces gated or secondary until activation/retention prove demand; keep web import APIs authenticated
validation: Feature flag audit; launch-surface tests; middleware production-gate test

finding: Photo pipeline, image caching, upload processing, and timeline scrub remain the highest measured performance-risk paths
evidence: Performance audit now targets non-empty timeline, dashboard timeline provider, progress-photo pipeline, and image-cache unit proof; frame/hitch proof still needs reliable runtime trace
recommended_action: Prioritize runtime trace and cache/fetch budget tests around photo HUD interactions
validation: Non-empty performance-audit unit cases; target-membered photo/cache coverage; trace

finding: Web offline/realtime sync now has focused queue and upload tests, but TODOs remain around echo prevention and daily metrics fetching
evidence: sync-manager.test.ts covers upload success, partial failure, offline logging, and cleanup; realtime-sync-manager.test.ts covers queued mutation success plus Supabase error retention
recommended_action: Keep web app scope constrained unless remaining realtime TODOs are resolved and covered

finding: Sentry and Statsig are optional in config, so launch monitoring proof needs explicit setup evidence
evidence: Release checklist treats SENTRY_DSN and STATSIG_CLIENT_SDK_KEY as optional but useful
recommended_action: Record whether production Sentry/Statsig are configured before launch claims
validation: Release config evidence; smoke event/crash capture if enabled

finding: Web account deletion page has historical evidence of simulation gap and must not be positioned as production deletion unless verified
evidence: App Store closeout docs note native and support deletion are launch surfaces while web settings account deletion needed follow-up
recommended_action: Verify current web deletion behavior or remove/redirect unfinished production web account deletion surface
validation: Route test; API delete-account integration test

finding: HealthKit allow, deny, skip, import, and sync paths are review-sensitive
evidence: Shared-lib source guard verifies production usage strings, HealthKit entitlement/project wiring, and release evidence requirements; interactive allow/deny/skip proof remains pending
recommended_action: Refresh simulator and device proof for HealthKit permission branches before App Store submission

finding: Audit outputs can drift unless regenerated from source after route, screen, or release-flow changes
evidence: This dossier is a static snapshot dated 2026-06-23; execution-status.md now carries a lightweight refresh checklist and PR #443 release evidence
recommended_action: Use the lightweight refresh checklist before release audits and after route/screen changes
validation: Route/screen counts compared to source; latest main CI/deploy/release-loop evidence refreshed
